3. Player goal (route cars, assign crews, keep deliveries flowing through rebalance) — present

didactic_fit: Strong. The playable mechanic directly exercises all three target concepts, not a reskin: L1 = predict partition via hash(key)%N (player clicks the predicted lane before the sim reveals truth); L2 = assign every lane to exactly one crew (consumer-group ownership, orphan/double-up detection); L3 = predict new owners after join/leave AND observe committed offsets survived (cursor keyed by partition, not consumer); L4 = rewind an offset cursor and predict exactly which messages re-deliver in order (replay). The headless sim core (src/sim/queue.ts) is a genuine partitioned-log implementation — partitionOf via stableHash (fnv1a+fmix32), append-only log with per-partition offsets, assignPartitions round-robin/range, rebalance carrying offsets verbatim, replay in offset order — proven by 12 vitest tests. The 3D scene renders the parallel-lane map that the PLAN argues a 2D layout cannot. Evidence is real and meaningful: pass=true only when routing/assignment/rebalance/replay invariants hold, with verifier_required=true routing the decision out of the game.
notes: ALL four-command gate green (lint+test+typecheck+build) AND smoke green (3/3, with the L1 EVIDENCE-assertion test passing).
Live smoke emits the correct voxeldojo schema (confirmed by the green spec assertions + emit.ts source).

CAVEAT (bookkeeping, not a gate failure): the output-dir evidence.json and screenshot.png are stale PixelDojo artifacts (source=pixelquest, schema 16_mini_message_queue-v1) from a different producer run, not this voxelDojo game's smoke output. The DONE-RULE is satisfied by the live voxelDojo smoke, not by those stale files. Consider regenerating them from this game's .logs/ to keep the output dir consistent.

U16-message-queue is not yet seeded in learner/learning_state.yaml (consistent with PLAN sec 12); the game correctly classifies attempts as 'deepening' via the static reviewSlice fallback and never writes learner state.
